english: 1. vb., pf. and fut. brṅon (brngon), to hunt, pursue, wild animals Cs., Sch.; to fish C. — 2. sbst. a hunter, huntsman Dzl. and Ler.; rṅón-pa-mo (rngon pa mo) a hunting woman, a huntress Cs.
Ld. to be able, v. rṅó-ba (rngo ba).

(cf. rṅám-pa (rngam pa) 1) splendour, stateliness, majesty; rṅóm-bag-can (rngom bag can), also col. *ṅóm-jìg-can* (*ngom jig can*), grand, majestic; terrible, of a judge, of terrifying deities. (A sbst. rṅóm-bag (rngom bag) = rṅóm-brjìd (rngom brjid) Cs. prob. does not exist.)

(Bal. *ṅa* (*nga*)), five, lṅá-bcu(-tam-pa) (lnga bcu(-tham pa)) fifty; lṅa-brgya (lnga brgya) five hundred; lṅá-bcu-rtsa-gcìg (lnga bcu rtsa gcig) (W. *ṅa-bcu-ṅa-cíg* (*nga bcu nga cig*)) or ṅa-gcìg (nga gcig), fifty one etc.; lṅá-pa (lnga pa) the fifth; lṅa-po (lnga po) consisting of five, cf. dgu (dgu); lṅá-ga (lnga ga) Cs., lṅá-ka (lnga ka) Pth. all the five, each of the five. The number five very often occurs in legends, as well as in sacred science, v. the Index to Burn. II., and to Fouc. Gyatch. II. under 'Cinq'. lṅa-lén (lnga len), Ssk. पञ्चाल, n. of a country in the north of ancient India.

(Ssk. पूर्व) a root signifying before, soon, early, rarely referring to space, and seldom used alone as adj. or adv., e.g. Dzl. 72V, 8: ₒdá-ba ni há-caṅ yaṅ sṅa cés-so ('da' ba ni ha cang yang snga ces so) deliverance (sc. from existence) takes place much too soon; ṅa ni tém-pa sṅa brgal yin (nga ni them pa snga brgal yin) I was the foremost, the first, to cross the threshold Glr.; bstán-pa sṅa dar bar dar pyi dar gsum (bstan pa snga dar bar dar phyi dar gsum) the first, intermediate, and last propagation of the doctrine Glr.; gen. it is used with an adjective termination, with postpositions, or in compounds. Deriv. sṅá-ba (snga ba) 1. adj. ancient, belonging or referring to former ages, rgya-nág-gi rgyál-po sṅá-ba (rgya nag gi rgyal po snga ba) an ancient king of China Glr.; of an early date, long ago, . . . las dá-lta sṅá-ba ₒdúg-gam (las da lta snga ba 'dug gam) is it already a long time, since . . . ? Mil.; 2. sbst. antiquity, the olden time; the morning; = sṅá-dro (snga dro), Mil.; 3. vb. pf. sṅas (sngas), to be the first, to come first, to be beforehand, (φθάνειν): rjé-yi sku mtoṅ ṅa sṅá-bas (rje yi sku mthong nga snga bas) as I was the first to see the king's face Glr.; gran-tsig sṅás-pa yin (gran tshig sngas pa yin) you were beforehand with me in disputing Glr.; *ṅa ṅe soṅ* (*nga nge song*) Sp. you promised it. — sṅá-bar (snga bar) in former times, formerly, in the morning; saṅ sṅá-bar (sang snga bar) to-morrow morning Glr. — sṅá-ma (snga ma) adj. 1. earlier, former, preceding, afore-said, frq.; sṅá-ma sṅá-ma (snga ma snga ma) always the anterior in time and place; sṅá-ma ltar (snga ma ltar), or bźín-du (bzhin du), as before, frq. 2. the first, the foremost in a series or succession Dzl.; ldáṅ-baï sṅá-ma (ldang ba'i snga ma) she who takes the first turn in getting up Mil. — sṅá-mo (snga mo) 1. earlier, by-gone; sṅá-mo-nas (snga mo nas) long ago Mil.; 2. W.: the morning, in the morning, *mā ṅá-mo* (*ma nga mo*) early in the morning, *tó-re ṅá-mo* (*to re nga mo*) to-morrow morning; also: early enough, in due time (opp. to *pí-mo* (*phi mo*)). — sṅá-ru (snga ru) v. sṅar (sngar), as a separate article. — sṅá-na (snga na) before, previously, (gen. sṅar (sngar) is used inst. of it). — sṅá-nas (snga nas) id., prop. of former times. Comp. sṅá-góṅ(-nas) (snga gong(-nas)) adv. before, previously, at first, a little while ago, just now Mil.; formerly, = late, deceased, sṅa-góṅ yab (snga gong yab) your late father Glr.; sṅa góṅ bód-kyi rgyál-po (snga gong bod kyi rgyal po) the earlier Tibetan kings Glr. — sṅa-dgóṅs (snga dgongs) morning and evening Sch. — sṅa-sṅá (snga snga) very early Sch. — sṅa-cád (snga chad) formerly, hitherto, till now, up to this time Dzl., = sṅan-cád (sngan chad), sṅon-cád (sngon chad). — sṅa-rtiṅ-du (snga rting du) earlier or later, not at the same time, e.g. brós-so (bros so) they escaped Glr. — sṅa-ltás (snga ltas) omen, presage, prognostic; also the fate or destiny portended. — sṅa-tóg (snga thog) 1. forenoon. 2. the first-fruits of harvest Cs. — sṅa-dús (snga dus) antiquity, time of old. — sṅa-dro (snga dro) the morning, the earlier part of the forenoon, 'the time before the heat of the sun'; sṅá-dro gcìg-la (snga dro gcig la) in half a forenoon Glr.; sṅá-dro dgóṅs-mo (snga dro dgongs mo) morning and evening Sch. v. above; saṅ sṅá-dro (sang snga dro) to-morrow morning Mil. — sṅa-pyí(r) (snga phyi(r)) sooner or later, like sṅa-rtiṅ-du (snga rting du) v. above Dzl. frq. — sṅá-rol (snga rol) time of old, past ages Cs.; sṅá-rol-tu (snga rol tu) before Tar. (cf. sṅón-rol (sngon rol)). — *ṅá-lo* (*nga lo*) W. last year. — sṅa śugs ₒdrén-pa (snga shugs 'dren pa) Cs.: 'the accenting of the first syllable'. — sṅa-sár (snga sar) early, sṅa-sar-sár (snga sar sar) very early Cs. — sṅa-sór (snga sor) 1. in the first place, first of all, at first (cf. rtiṅ-sor (rting sor)) Glr. 2. anciently, in old times Cs.
